lines.gurmukhi: ਸੂਹੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ਗੁਣਵੰਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: soohee mahalaa 5 gunavantee |
lines.english: Soohee, Fifth Mehl, Gunvantee ~ The Worthy And Virtuous Bride:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਦੀਸੈ ਗੁਰਸਿਖੜਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਨਿਵਿ ਨਿਵਿ ਲਾਗਉ ਪਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: jo deesai gurasikharraa tis niv niv laagau paae jeeo |
lines.english: When I see a Sikh of the Guru, I humbly bow and fall at his feet.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਖਾ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਜੀਅ ਕੀ ਗੁਰੁ ਸਜਣੁ ਦੇਹਿ ਮਿਲਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: aakhaa birathaa jeea kee gur sajan dehi milaae jeeo |
lines.english: I tell to him the pain of my soul, and beg him to unite me with the Guru, my Best Friend.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਈ ਦਸਿ ਉਪਦੇਸੜਾ ਮੇਰਾ ਮਨੁ ਅਨਤ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਜਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: soee das upadesarraa meraa man anat na kaahoo jaae jeeo |
lines.english: I ask that he impart to me such an understanding, that my mind will not go out wandering anywhere else.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਆਇਆ ਦੂਰਹੁ ਚਲਿ ਕੈ ਮੈ ਤਕੀ ਤਉ ਸਰਣਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: hau aaeaa doorahu chal kai mai takee tau saranaae jeeo |
lines.english: I have come so far, seeking the Protection of Your Sanctuary.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਤੈ ਕੂੰ ਡੇਵਸਾ ਮੈ ਮਾਰਗੁ ਦੇਹੁ ਬਤਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: eihu man tai koon ddevasaa mai maarag dehu bataae jeeo |
lines.english: I dedicate this mind to you. Please, show me the Path to God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਤੁ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਚਲੇ ਭਾਈਅੜੇ ਗੁਰੁ ਕਹੈ ਸੁ ਕਾਰ ਕਮਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: eit maarag chale bhaaeearre gur kahai su kaar kamaae jeeo |
lines.english: So walk on this Path, O sister soul-brides; do that work which the Guru tells you to do.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਆਸਾ ਰਖੀ ਚਿਤਿ ਮਹਿ ਮੇਰਾ ਸਭੋ ਦੁਖੁ ਗਵਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: mai aasaa rakhee chit meh meraa sabho dukh gavaae jeeo |
lines.english: Within my mind, I place my hopes in You; please, take my pain and suffering away!

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਆਗੇਂ ਮਨ ਕੀ ਮਤੜੀ ਵਿਸਾਰੇਂ ਦੂਜਾ ਭਾਉ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: tiaagen man kee matarree visaaren doojaa bhaau jeeo |
lines.english: Abandon the intellectual pursuits of the mind, and forget the love of duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਉ ਪਾਵਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਦਰਸਾਵੜਾ ਨਹ ਲਗੈ ਤਤੀ ਵਾਉ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: eiau paaveh har darasaavarraa neh lagai tatee vaau jeeo |
lines.english: In this way, you shall obtain the Blessed Vision of the Lord's Darshan; the hot winds shall not even touch you.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਆਪਹੁ ਬੋਲਿ ਨ ਜਾਣਦਾ ਮੈ ਕਹਿਆ ਸਭੁ ਹੁਕਮਾਉ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: hau aapahu bol na jaanadaa mai kahiaa sabh hukamaau jeeo |
lines.english: By myself, I do not even know how to speak; I speak all that the Lord commands.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਖਜਾਨਾ ਬਖਸਿਆ ਗੁਰਿ ਨਾਨਕਿ ਕੀਆ ਪਸਾਉ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: har bhagat khajaanaa bakhasiaa gur naanak keea pasaau jeeo |
lines.english: I am blessed with the treasure of the Lord's devotional worship; Guru Nanak has been kind and compassionate to me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈ ਬਹੁੜਿ ਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਭੁਖੜੀ ਹਉ ਰਜਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਅਘਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: mai bahurr na trisanaa bhukharree hau rajaa tripat aghaae jeeo |
lines.english: I shall never again feel hunger or thirst; I am satisfied, satiated and fulfilled.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਗੁਰ ਦੀਸੈ ਸਿਖੜਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਨਿਵਿ ਨਿਵਿ ਲਾਗਉ ਪਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jo gur deesai sikharraa tis niv niv laagau paae jeeo |3|
lines.english: When I see a Sikh of the Guru, I humbly bow and fall at his feet. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਰਿ ਜੋਬਨਿ ਮੈ ਮਤ ਪੇਈਅੜੈ ਘਰਿ ਪਾਹੁਣੀ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhar joban mai mat peeearrai ghar paahunee bal raam jeeo |
lines.english: Intoxicated with the wine of youth, I did not realize that I was only a guest at my parents' home (in this world).

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਸੂਹੀ ਛੰਤ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਘਰੁ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: raag soohee chhant mahalaa 1 ghar 1 |
lines.english: Raag Soohee, Chhant, First Mehl, First House:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਜਾਣੀ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਣੀ ਜੋਬਨੁ ਬਾਦਿ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: gun saar na jaanee bharam bhulaanee joban baad gavaaeaa |
lines.english: I have not known the value of virtue; I have been deluded by doubt. I have wasted away my youth in vain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈਲੀ ਅਵਗਣਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਗੁਣ ਨ ਸਮਾਵਨੀ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: mailee avagan chit bin gur gun na samaavanee bal raam jeeo |
lines.english: My consciousness is polluted with faults and mistakes; without the Guru, virtue does not even enter into me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਰੁ ਘਰੁ ਦਰੁ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਨਹੀ ਜਾਤਾ ਪਿਰ ਕਾ ਸਹਜੁ ਨ ਭਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: var ghar dar darasan nahee jaataa pir kaa sehaj na bhaaeaa |
lines.english: I have not known my Husband Lord, His celestial home and gate, or the Blessed Vision of His Darshan. I have not had the pleasure of my Husband Lord's celestial peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਬਾਲਤਣਿ ਰਾਡੇਪਾ ਬਿਨੁ ਪਿਰ ਧਨ ਕੁਮਲਾਣੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak baalatan raaddepaa bin pir dhan kumalaanee |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, in the prime of my youth, I am a widow; without my Husband Lord, the soul-bride is wasting away. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪੂਛਿ ਨ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਚਾਲੀ ਸੂਤੀ ਰੈਣਿ ਵਿਹਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: satigur poochh na maarag chaalee sootee rain vihaanee |
lines.english: After consulting the True Guru, I have not walked on the Path; the night of my life is passing away in sleep.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਬਾ ਮੈ ਵਰੁ ਦੇਹਿ ਮੈ ਹਰਿ ਵਰੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਿਸ ਕੀ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: baabaa mai var dehi mai har var bhaavai tis kee bal raam jeeo |
lines.english: O father, give me in marriage to the Lord; I am pleased with Him as my Husband. I belong to Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਵਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਜੁਗ ਚਾਰਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਣ ਬਾਣੀ ਜਿਸ ਕੀ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: rav rahiaa jug chaar tribhavan baanee jis kee bal raam jeeo |
lines.english: He is pervading throughout the four ages, and the Word of His Bani permeates the three worlds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਣ ਕੰਤੁ ਰਵੈ ਸੋਹਾਗਣਿ ਅਵਗਣਵੰਤੀ ਦੂਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: tribhavan kant ravai sohaagan avaganavantee doore |
lines.english: The Husband Lord of the three worlds ravishes and enjoys His virtuous brides, but He keeps the ungraceful and unvirtuous ones far away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੀ ਆਸਾ ਤੈਸੀ ਮਨਸਾ ਪੂਰਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਭਰਪੂਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: jaisee aasaa taisee manasaa poor rahiaa bharapoore |
lines.english: As are our hopes, so are our minds' desires, which the All-pervading Lord brings to fulfillment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮੈ ਵਰੁ ਸਾਚਾ ਭਾਵੈ ਜੁਗਿ ਜੁਗਿ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਤੈਸੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak mai var saachaa bhaavai jug jug preetam taise |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, I love my True Husband Lord; my Beloved is the same, age after age. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੀ ਨਾਰਿ ਸੁ ਸਰਬ ਸੁਹਾਗਣਿ ਰਾਂਡ ਨ ਮੈਲੈ ਵੇਸੇ ॥
lines.translit: har kee naar su sarab suhaagan raandd na mailai vese |
lines.english: The bride of the Lord is forever happy and virtuous; she shall never be a widow, and she shall never have to wear dirty clothes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਾ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਰਜਾਇ ਸੋ ਨ ਟਲੈ ਜੋ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਕਰੈ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: saahaa hukam rajaae so na ttalai jo prabh karai bal raam jeeo |
lines.english: The moment of that marriage will be set by the Hukam of God's Command; His Will cannot be changed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਬਾ ਲਗਨੁ ਗਣਾਇ ਹੰ ਭੀ ਵੰਞਾ ਸਾਹੁਰੈ ਬਲਿ ਰਾਮ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: baabaa lagan ganaae han bhee vanyaa saahurai bal raam jeeo |
lines.english: O Baba, calculate that auspicious moment, when I too shall be going to my in-laws' house.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਰਤੁ ਪਇਆ ਕਰਤੈ ਕਰਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਮੇਟਿ ਨ ਸਕੈ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: kirat peaa karatai kar paaeaa mett na sakai koee |
lines.english: The karmic record of past deeds, written by the Creator Lord, cannot be erased by anyone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਞੀ ਨਾਉ ਨਰਹ ਨਿਹਕੇਵਲੁ ਰਵਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਤਿਹੁ ਲੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jaayee naau narah nihakeval rav rahiaa tihu loee |
lines.english: The most respected member of the marriage party, my Husband, is the independent Lord of all beings, pervading and permeating the three worlds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇ ਨਿਰਾਸੀ ਰੋਇ ਵਿਛੁੰਨੀ ਬਾਲੀ ਬਾਲੈ ਹੇਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: maae niraasee roe vichhunee baalee baalai hete |
lines.english: Maya, crying out in pain, leaves, seeing that the bride and the groom are in love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਾਚ ਸਬਦਿ ਸੁਖ ਮਹਲੀ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਣੀ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਚੇਤੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: naanak saach sabad sukh mahalee gur charanee prabh chete |3|
lines.english: O Nanak, the peace of the Mansion of God's Presence comes through the True Word of the Shabad; the bride keeps the Guru's Feet enshrined in her mind. ||3||
